title: Davallia tristis
taxonomy: Davallia tristis belongs to the family Davalliaceae. It is a species of fern in the genus Davallia. Davallia tristis is native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The classification of Davallia tristis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, and Family: Davalliaceae. Davallia tristis is a species of terrestrial fern that grows in shaded areas and mountainous regions.

intro: Davallia tristis, also known as the Japanese holly fern or Japanese chain fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Davallia tristis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Davallia tristis is commonly used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine and Japanese herbal remedies.
